cookie_message: This site uses no tracking cookies. We respect your digital sovereignty.
hero_subtitle: DAIGU TECHNOLOGY — Integrated Ecosystem of Science, Software, and Industry.
hero_quote: "From the Atom to the Software, everything is designed, built, and secured here."
hero_alignment: Aligned with the New Quality Productive Forces — 15th Five-Year Plan
vision_wrong_desc: Perpetual dependency on extraterritorial licenses and decisions. The Huawei-Google lesson.
vision_right_desc: Software first, hardware second. No DAIGU terminal ships without its sovereign OS.
eco_dlc_desc: R&D Center, Digital Twin, AI, Rapid Prototyping. The Convergence Projects Office.
eco_tech_desc: Strategic Holding. Subsidiary capital ownership, patent protection, long-term steering (2030-2050).
eco_duts_desc: DUTS — Training Guinean technical elites. Here, the student is a co-creator of national infrastructure.
filiales_electronics_desc: Hardware & Integration — Drones, Rugged Tablets, Network Equipment. "TROPIC-PROOF" design.
filiales_core_desc: Software & AI — DAIGU OS (Micro-Kernel), Agentic AI, Digital Twin Engine.
filiales_cloud_desc: Cyber & Infra — Guinean National Cloud, Internal Red Team, Zero Trust Security, Offline Resilience.
filiales_energy_desc: Materials & Batteries — Stationary storage, Bauxite residue valorization, Solar micro-grids.
formation_intro: The Talent Factory. No disconnected theoretical courses: every student works on a real DAIGU subsidiary project, supervised by DLC engineers.
map_fallback: 🇬🇳 Republic of Guinea — Conakry, DAIGU Project Headquarters
roadmap_phase0_desc: Legal constitution of the Holding, opening of the first DUTS cohort, DLC land acquisition.
roadmap_phase1_desc: Release of DAIGU OS Lite V0.1. Launch of the Guinean Private Cloud. First convergence projects.
roadmap_phase2_desc: Assembly of the first autonomous terminal: rugged tablet running DAIGU OS. Pilot battery production.
roadmap_phase3_desc: Regional Cloud expansion. DAIGU OS on TV and vehicles. DUTS opens to the sub-region.
faq_q1: Why not use Android or Linux?
faq_a1: Android without Google Mobile Services is unusable internationally. Linux depends on build chains and repositories hosted outside Guinea. DAIGU OS is a sovereign kernel, self-hosted, designed for our specific needs.
faq_a2: DAIGU TECHNOLOGY is a Guinean-law holding company, controlled by a locked Endowment Fund guaranteeing the reinvestment of profits into training (DUTS) and research (DLC).
faq_q3: How does DAIGU fit with the Simandou project?
faq_a3: DAIGU ENERGY LABS works on mining residue valorization and energy storage solutions for critical infrastructure. DAIGU CLOUD & SHIELD can secure mining operators' data.
faq_q4: When will the first products be available?
faq_a4: Phase 1 (OS and Cloud) is planned over 2 to 4 years. The first physical terminals running DAIGU OS are expected in Phase 2 (Year 4-7). Software sovereignty takes priority over rapid hardware release.
faq_q5: How to become a partner or investor?
faq_a5: Contact our Architecture Committee via the form at the bottom of the page. We study partnerships aligned with our sovereignty doctrine and roadmap.
resource_vision_desc: Non-negotiable constraints per subsidiary. PDF, 8 pages.
resource_filiales_desc: Organization chart, missions, flagship products. PDF, 1 page A3.
team_brain_quote: "I am not building for tomorrow. I am building for 30 years from now."
team_cyber_quote: "Security added after the fact is not security. It is a bandage."
team_energy_quote: "Energy must never be the reason an idea dies."
team_os_quote: "A kernel is not a technical detail. It is an act of sovereignty."
team_hardware_quote: "The terminal is but the software's case. It must survive its environment."

blog_post1_excerpt: Digital sovereignty is not negotiable. It is compiled. A look back at DAIGU CORE SYSTEMS' founding decision.
blog_post2_title: The Simandou lesson: Train before extracting
blog_post2_excerpt: Africa's largest mining project teaches us one truth: without skill transfer, there is no sustainable development.
blog_post3_title: Cybersecurity in the tropics: the ignored challenges
blog_post3_excerpt: Why Western security standards fail between 30°C and 90% humidity, and how DAIGU responds.
dashboard_note: Simulation — Real metrics activated in Phase 1.
contact_intro: The DAIGU Project is carried by private Guinean interests and open to bilateral cooperation within the framework of Luban Workshops and FOCAC.
footer_quote: "In Vestri Manus Est — It is in your hands."
